Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to produce a Python dictionary for temporal profile of activities in a supply chain process. The activities: SS, OP, RC, QI, CA, PT, PK, WS, DT, AS. We need pairs of activities that eventually follow each other in some trace, not just directly.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Typically, times in seconds? Could be days, hours, etc. Lets think typical supply chain: supplier selection might take days, order placement quick, receive components weeks, inspection days, assembly days, testing days, packaging hours, warehouse storage days to months, distribution days, after-sales support months? But we need standard deviations as well.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Well use seconds for consistency. But can use hours or days converted to seconds. Lets assume times in seconds. For example: - SS to OP: average 1 day = 86400 sec, std dev 1 hour = 3600. - OP to RC: average 2 weeks = 1209600, std dev 2 days = 172800. [all direct pairs listed]

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now non-direct pairs: e.g., SS to RC: average time from SS to RC is SSOP + OPRC = maybe 864001209600=1296000, std dev combine? Assuming independence, sqrt(3600^2172800^2) 172800? ... Similarly SS to QI: SSOPOPRCRCQ I = sum of times: 86400120960086400=1382400, std dev? sqrt(3600^2172800^214400^2) 173000?

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RC to CA: Actually RCQI CA = 8640086400=172800 sec = 2 days, std dev sqrt(14400^221600^2)= sqrt(207360000466560000)= sqrt(673920000)=25960. So (172800, 25960) ... similar detailed calcs for others like QI to PT, CA to PK, etc.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thats plenty. We can also include some non-temporal like SS to AS already done. ... The temporal profile is for ordered pairs (A,B) where A precedes B. So we dont need reverse pairs. So all above are fine.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Now need to output dictionary. Well generate a list of tuples with these pairs. We must ensure keys are tuples (string, string). Use uppercase as given. ... Well include all pairs above. Use consistent spacing.
